three_layer_analysis.l1_daily: BEARISH. Since Jun 15/22 high, structure is predominantly LH+LL. Bearish SMT at top confirmed (NDX swept BSL, SPX did not). 4 consecutive bearish D1 candles Mon-Thu. Jun 25 opened at the high (29,843.89 = high) and sold off -404 pts — strong bearish conviction. Price at 50.8% of D1 range (28,197→30,643) — equilibrium zone. Jun 24→25 showed HH+HL structure but candle closed bearish — this is NOT a bullish reversal, it's a lower-timeframe bounce within a bearish macro structure.
three_layer_analysis.l2_h4_context: BEARISH. H4 dealing range: 29,160.50 (today's low) → 30,263.75 (Jun 25 evening high) = 1,103 pts. Price at 17.3% of H4 range — deep in discount. Today's session: pre-market rally to 29,922.50 (04:00) then sustained selling to 29,160.50 (11:30 ET). Bounced to 29,562.75 (15:30) then drifting lower to 29,351. Today's low (29,160.50) was within 29 pts of D1 61.8% Fib (29,131.15) — key confluence level tested and held. H4 structure: predominantly LH+LL since Jun 22 evening. The bounce from 29,160 is a retracement within the bearish H4 range.

weekly_profile.details: Monday established the week high (30,642.57). Tuesday gapped down -978 pts and continued selling. Wednesday made the week low (28,963.10). Thursday opened above Wednesday close but sold off hard (-404 pts, opened at high). This is a classic bearish week: Mon high → Tue/Wed expansion → Thu continuation. Friday profile: likely continuation or mild retracement. After 1,680-pt week range, some profit-taking possible but bias remains firmly bearish.

reasoning: Strong bearish bias across all timeframes. D1 structure is LH+LL with bearish SMT at the top (NDX swept BSL at Jun 22 high while SPX failed to confirm — classic ICT divergence). 4 consecutive bearish daily candles with Thursday opening at the high and selling off — maximum bearish conviction. Price at D1 50% Fib (29,419.74) — equilibrium zone. Today's session confirmed bearish continuation: pre-market Judas rally to 29,922.50 followed by sustained selling to 29,160.50 (within 29 pts of D1 61.8% Fib). The 61.8% level held, suggesting possible short-term support, but the overall bias remains bearish. H4 range broken to the downside — price at 17.3% of H4 range (deep discount). Key question: will D1 61.8% (29,131) hold as support for a retracement, or will it break targeting 70.5% (28,918) and 79% (28,710)? Given the bearish SMT and weekly profile, any bounce is likely a selling opportunity. Confidence 65/100 — strong bearish signals but at 61.8% Fib confluence which could trigger a bounce. Entry requires M15 confirmation in NY AM Kill Zone.
